note: Hard, open-ended, high-value tasks. Alias gpt-5.6 routes here. Codex sub usage is near-free.
Everyday work, read-heavy subagents (exploration, triage, summarization).
Clear, repeatable high-volume tasks: log digging, bulk analysis, mechanical migrations.
Orchestrator. Best-in-class intelligence and taste; use for steering, reviews, user-facing output.
Reviews and second passes; often cheaper than token-hungry sonnet-5 in practice.
Avoid for real work (video: never use haiku, especially with Codex near-free).

learning: Codex-MCP: gpt-5.6-Modelle von installierter Codex-Version abgelehnt (Upgrade noetig); Lauf mit gpt-5.5 starb am MCP-Timeout ohne Output. Thunderbird-Signatur-Konfig danach direkt vom Orchestrator erledigt (prefs.js, 2 Keys). Fuer laengere Codex-Laeufe CLI-detached statt blocking MCP nutzen.

Terra fetched+cleaned a YouTube auto-transcript (yt-dlp) and filed it with good header/slug unprompted details. MCP transport hit the 60s bridge cap but the run completed server-side; verify via filesystem instead of rerunning.
Codex MCP timed out twice (harness -32001) on a read-only commit review from Cowork sandbox; use detached CLI or longer harness timeout for such runs.
